gaps.statement: Project Model does not yet reject latent and concrete Module owner or ungoverned disposition conflicts, prove total tracked-path classification, or bind Context Capsule write scope to the resulting opaque ownership product.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before automatic worker write capabilities are enabled.
gaps.resolution: Project Model now compiles one opaque exact-Model-and-tracked-path-facts-bound ownership product, rejects incomplete or conflicting tracked-path coverage and malformed ownership policy, and projects the same authoritative and caller-narrowed write decisions into Context Capsule and Kernel lifecycle enforcement.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if tracked-path assignment can be incomplete or ambiguous, ownership or disposition policy can bypass the restricted selector grammar, the opaque product can be forged or reused across source drift, or Context and Kernel write decisions stop consuming the same product.

gaps.statement: Minimum Gate commands are selected by primary Module, but are not yet pruned further by exact Verification Obligations or Contract deltas.
gaps.expansionTrigger: When a Module's focused command stops being the smallest high-confidence route for routine Changes.

gaps.statement: Exit streams, signals, and byte truncation are characterized locally; timeout abort, encoding boundaries, and cross-platform subprocess behavior are not.
gaps.resolution: Assurance Runtime now declares local-direct-node-posix-v1, observes independent bounded raw-byte streams through confirmed close, distinguishes support, control, exit, signal, truncation, and strict UTF-8 facts, and reports every unsupported, malformed, controlled, or unconfirmed case as non-positive; Change Kernel consumes those facts without recompiling process semantics.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if the declared support descriptor broadens without a fixed conformance proof, raw-byte or close facts become lossy, timeout or cancellation can return positive before settlement, unsupported or malformed observations can pass, or Kernel and Runtime diverge on Command Observation meaning.

gaps.statement: The browser projection has no DOM-level test proving that it preserves Project Model and Change semantics.
gaps.resolution: The fixed Local Workbench proof now drives a loopback HTTP Profile window through the browser adapter and a real DOM: a successor replaces rather than accumulates the current page, cross-snapshot and malformed envelopes fail closed without replacing it, stale continuation errors redact the opaque token, and late responses cannot overwrite a newer generation.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if the DOM workflow no longer executes through loopback HTTP and the production adapter, if a successor can append or replace across source mismatch, if continuation material can be decoded or rendered, or if late responses can overwrite a newer page.

gaps.statement: The only Kernel Profile query compiles every Change record and every referenced frozen Governance Baseline into one result under one 2 MiB and aggregate route-query budget; the repository's real accepted history already exceeds that shape, and there is no authenticated snapshot-bound continuation or adapter traversal proof.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before LGT-014 implementation begins or any Profile history beyond one legal window is presented.
gaps.resolution: Change Kernel now selects a canonical Change-id window from one stabilized composite source before Profile facts or historical route Providers are compiled, enforces fixed record and output ceilings, and issues process-authenticated source-bound continuations. The pure Local Workbench view model plus strict CLI, loopback HTTP, and browser controller preserve each window and traverse without retaining full history or recompiling governance semantics.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if any Profile read materializes unselected history before windowing, repeats source observation per record, emits beyond fixed ceilings, accepts malformed, modified, expired, unissued, cross-process, cross-snapshot, or cross-query continuations, writes the Store during reads, or lets adapters decode cursors, merge snapshots, accumulate all pages, or recompile governance semantics.

gaps.statement: The canonical Workbench projection owns Module, Claim, route, and Change-action semantics, but it does not yet project Plan authoring options or acceptance-input requirements; the browser still filters Plan choices from Project facts and submits opaque authority, decision-type, Closure, and amendment inputs for Kernel validation.
gaps.expansionTrigger: The LGT-014 boundary was crossed without closing this Gap. LGT-019 must close it before Local Workbench becomes governed or any further Workbench authoring or acceptance surface expands.
gaps.resolution: Change Kernel now compiles the complete bounded Plan-authoring and selected-Change acceptance-input requirement product from one stabilized composite Model, Git, and Store snapshot, and rechecks the exact requirements digest plus four-field human confirmation binding before applying Closure or Authority input. Local Workbench strictly receives and freezes the nested projection, renders only its Change kinds, Outcome cardinality, Claims, routes, Decision options, Closure modes, exact references, and disabled reasons, and submits the confirmed binding unchanged; CLI and browser Profile failures remain complete, bounded, and non-aggregate.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if Workbench input requirements stop sharing one stabilized source with Claim, route, and action semantics; if acceptance can apply Closure or Decision input without an exact current confirmation match; if adapters inspect raw Plan, Project Model, Gate, scope, or Change internals to invent eligibility; if Profile failure output becomes partial, unbounded, cursor-leaking, or aggregate; or if the browser can mutate a received canonical projection.

gaps.statement: The Development Plan and Change alignment are machine-readable, but no bounded, snapshot-consistent Project Atlas profile yet projects Outcome to Claim, Gate, current or historical Evidence, Residual Uncertainty, and Knowledge Gap from their existing fact authorities.
gaps.resolution: Project Model compiles exact typed relations, Change Kernel emits them from one stabilized composite source snapshot with orthogonal Evidence currency, and Local Workbench exposes one bounded non-aggregate Profile view model through CLI, loopback HTTP, and the browser adapter.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if Profile relation identity, stabilized source binding, Evidence currency separation, bounded references, or non-aggregate Local Workbench projection is weakened.

gaps.statement: The Change Store supplies stable bounded zero-write Profile snapshots; Project Model supplies the accepted opaque exact-Model-bound Claim Gate route product; and Change Kernel now consumes one current-Model product, at most one query-demand union product per verified frozen Governance Baseline, and one aggregate query budget. The remaining projection boundaries are declared but not implemented: Project Model must enforce an append-only blocker relation from this later-discovered prerequisite without rewriting frozen LGT-010 Criteria; Kernel must emit complete selectable owned or dependency Contract Claims, exact routes, and closed-code Change-action disabled reasons from the same stabilized query and current route product; Local Workbench must compile and adopt one pure bounded view model; and browser compatibility code can still express aggregate score, confidence, percentage, health, readiness, or green-light semantics.
gaps.expansionTrigger: First implement structured Project Model enforcement that makes this open Gap block architecture-acceptance-profile-not-projected closure and the LGT-010 Transition without rewriting frozen Criteria; then implement both proof Claims, adopt the pure Local Workbench view model, and remove aggregate assurance compatibility.
gaps.resolution: Kernel now emits one bounded Workbench semantic projection from a stable source and Project Model route products, while Local Workbench consumes source-bound actions and a pure bounded Profile view model; aggregate assurance compatibility has been removed from the browser surface.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if browser or adapter code recompiles Contract visibility, Gate applicability, lifecycle action policy, aggregate assurance, or cross-snapshot eligibility.

gaps.statement: Project Model owns the opaque exact-Model-bound Claim Gate route product and Change Kernel emits the bounded Workbench projection, but authoring Contract visibility and acceptance eligibility are still recomputed inside Kernel rather than consumed through one Project Model bounded batch Interface, leaving a semantic drift point before Local Workbench adoption.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Implement the Project Model batch projection first, then refactor Change Kernel to consume it exclusively before Local Workbench adapters adopt the Workbench projection.
gaps.resolution: Project Model owns one exact-Model-bound bounded batch projection for Module-visible Claims and acceptance routes, and Change Kernel consumes that projection exclusively for current and unique frozen baselines without traversing Contract or Gate semantics.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if Kernel regains Contract visibility, Gate scope, command scope, effective-Module filtering, or per-record Project Model projection semantics.

gaps.statement: Later-discovered prerequisites cannot yet block exact Knowledge Gap closures or Outcome Transitions through an append-only structured relation, so prose sequencing can be bypassed without rewriting frozen Outcome Criteria.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Implement and directly prove Project Model validation, append-only preservation, cycle rejection, transitive required-disposition derivation, atomic blocker closure, and exact route blocking before the Kernel Workbench semantics slice.
gaps.resolution: Project Model and Outcome Transition compilation now validate append-only acyclic blocker relations, derive direct and transitive baseline-open prerequisites, and require every blocker Closure Contract to compile atomically in the exact consuming Transition.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if blocker validation, append-only history, transitive derivation, route blocking, or atomic Closure Contract consumption is weakened.

gaps.statement: Accepted Change history lives only in Git-ignored local runtime state, so a fresh clone or another workspace cannot reproduce the historical Evidence portion of an Architecture Acceptance Profile from versioned repository content alone.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before Architecture Acceptance Profiles are exchanged across clones or workspaces, used for remote review, or relied on as durable audit artifacts.

gaps.statement: Ordinary Changes now bind exact Claims visible through the primary Module's owned or dependency Contracts to stable active Outcome Criteria; unresolved or ambiguous matches fail closed, and explicit non-progress exceptions require frozen Development Plan authority.
gaps.resolution: Enforced compilation derives content-addressed Outcome Contributions, and the Kernel re-derives their acceptance-bound projection from the frozen Governance Baseline; exceptions grant no progress and cannot prove transitions. Contributions prove relevance, not Criterion satisfaction.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Exact Contribution derivation, enforced rejection, Plan-authority routing, or acceptance-time rederivation is weakened.

gaps.statement: Development Outcome activation, achievement, and conditional activation now compile from a Candidate-frozen catalog of prior sealed Accepted Packages, exact Contribution and Gate Evidence bindings, closed Gap dispositions, and explicit Plan-authority Criterion assessments.
gaps.resolution: The Project Model owns deterministic route and proof sufficiency semantics; the Kernel freezes and content-addresses the prior Package catalog, resolves Store records, binds the compiler projection into the Verification Subject and Accepted Package, and re-runs resolution before Gates and acceptance. Declared mode remains observable for adopters, while this repository enforces missing or incomplete Transitions.
gaps.reopenTrigger: The Candidate catalog can be regenerated after creation; historical Package seals, exact Contribution or Gate Evidence bindings, Gap dispositions, authority assessments, Gate-time re-resolution, or acceptance-time re-resolution are weakened.

gaps.statement: Workers have no capability sandbox and authority identities are local unauthenticated assertions.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before remote workers, multi-user acceptance, or signed attestations.

gaps.statement: The provider-neutral Worker Execution values and pure append-only lifecycle are declared but do not yet have one fixed conformance proof that rejects semantic, authority, digest-chain, and resource-boundary attacks.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before LGT-020 can become achieved or any worker Adapter consumes the Interface.
gaps.resolution: A sealed Worker Execution implementation Package plus its independently sealed reconstruction-hardening Package expose one deep provider-neutral protocol facade and one fixed deterministic conformance scenario that proves live and serialized lifecycle validation while rejecting authority injection, provider leakage, duplicate identity, cross-record binding, digest-chain, replay, scope-escalation, Context self-grant, contradiction, truncation, dynamic-limit, and resource-boundary attacks.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if live application and serialized reconstruction stop enforcing the same canonical closed value boundary, fixed semantic-attack proof, unique event identity, exact prior-record and source bindings, dynamic Capability Profile limits, digest-chain lifecycle, capability-fact separation, Context request non-grant semantics, or non-authority boundary.

gaps.statement: No declared local execution profile independently proves that filesystem, process, network, or secret capability limits are enforced at the worker execution boundary rather than merely observed or requested.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before LGT-022 is activated or any capability profile is described as enforced.

gaps.statement: No fixed proof binds one stable opaque Assurance Runtime source-byte product to a logical compiled Context Capsule and exact materialization, derives neutral Context Impact from frozen Project Model sources, or proves that only an unforgeable process-local controller Session product can append a recorded-before-disclosure successor Context.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before LGT-021 is achieved or any Worker Adapter receives a Context materialization.

gaps.statement: Local Authority decidedBy values are unauthenticated assertions without a signed identity binding or independent identity provider fact.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before remote workers, multi-user acceptance, or signed attestations rely on Authority identity.

gaps.statement: Legatura self-hosts its Project Model, but no materialized app/apk/relay reference directly proves exact incremental ownership and assurance boundaries, one ordinary relay Change through the unchanged Kernel lifecycle, and faithful projection through the existing CLI without whole-repository modeling or inferred completeness.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before Legatura is presented as a reusable framework for an existing software repository.
gaps.resolution: A fixed versioned app/apk/relay reference now compiles through the ordinary Project Model with exact ownership and assurance boundaries, exercises one relay-only Change through the unchanged Kernel lifecycle to an exact Accepted Package, and projects the same bounded facts through the existing inspect --json CLI without whole-repository inference or aggregate assurance.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if the versioned reference no longer validates exact total ownership and explicit provisional or opaque boundaries, if brownfield Changes gain lifecycle or acceptance exceptions, if dependency implementation enters initial relay Context, if reference Gaps are hidden or inferred closed, or if existing CLI inspection loses exact facts or introduces adoption-specific or aggregate assurance conclusions.

gaps.statement: The Kernel projects generic Module Claim eligibility plus protected Claims by Outcome, so the browser still intersects them into final integrity-repair eligibility; browser receivers check digest shape without verifying projection content; and the governed disabled-reason vocabulary is behind the runtime closed set. The trusted loopback server still revalidates every mutation and the pure server Profile compiler rejects aggregate assurance, so this residual is projection integrity rather than an acceptance-authority bypass.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before LGT-016 is activated or any Workbench creation or Architecture Profile browser surface expands.
gaps.resolution: Change Kernel now emits one bounded terminal Claim-selection product from the same stabilized composite snapshot, Plan authoring policy, and Project Model Claim-route product that govern Change creation; browser adapters receive only exact lookup routes, independently verify canonical Workbench and Profile content digests, reject aggregate assurance and malformed projection attacks, and never reconstruct protected-Claim or eligibility policy.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if adapters regain raw protected-Claim sets or combine Plan, Contract, Gate, lifecycle, or Module facts into eligibility; if any browser projection becomes state without exact canonical content verification; if malformed route or disabled-reason vocabularies are accepted; or if Profile surfaces introduce aggregate confidence, score, percentage, health, readiness, or green-light conclusions.

gaps.statement: The Kernel permanently freezes every declared Outcome statement, so speculative planned or conditional meanings cannot yet be revised through append-only history before activation.
gaps.expansionTrigger: Before a planned or conditional Outcome needs semantic refinement or any S2 Outcome is activated.
gaps.resolution: Project Model now compiles a bounded append-only Revision ledger with complete prior definitions, exact digests, compiler-derived changed fields, frozen Plan authority, Revision/Transition isolation, and activation bindings; Change Kernel rederives the same Interface before Gates and acceptance.
gaps.reopenTrigger: Reopen if Revision history can be deleted, rewritten, reordered, forged, mixed with a status Transition, applied after activation, or accepted without Kernel lifecycle rederivation.
